summary.brief: Medical malpractice case involving surgery orthopaedic.

evidence_index.text: The defendant, Mr Cope, is a consultant orthopaedic surgeon specialising in lower limb arthroplasty.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mr Cope performed a right total hip replacement on Mr Bradfield-Kay on 6 November 2009.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mr Cope performed the left total hip replacement on 18 December 2009.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Mr Chatterji stated that it was a straightforward procedure to remove the cup and re-orientate it intraoperatively if it was found to be prominent.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: Mr Manktelow, though critical of the orientation of the acetabular component, would not describe that in its own right as constituting a breach of duty.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

evidence_index.text: I find the allegations of breach of duty at paragraph 48(1) of the Particulars of Claim have been made out.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The allegation at paragraph 48(2) of the Particulars of Claim has not been established.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: I reject Mr Cope's evidence that he undertook an examination of Mr Bradfield-Kay.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: Mr Cope saw Mr Bradfield-Kay again on 7 February 2011.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The experts agreed that the ideal orientation of the acetabular component in any given case is an ongoing challenge for hip surgeons.
evidence_index.speaker: Expert witness

initial_state.chief_complaint: Severe pain in the left thigh and groin
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Mr Bradfield-Kay developed serious pain in the thigh and groin immediately after a left total hip replacement performed on 18 December 2009.
past_medical_history: Complex history of back and leg pain

focused_exam.musculoskeletal: Examination findings not specified in judgment

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Post-operative pain following left total hip replacement
critical_findings: Pain in thigh and groin post-surgery

requestables.name: X-ray of the left hip
requestables.description: Radiographic images to assess the position of the acetabular component

reveal.result_summary: Difference in version in the right and left hips; left acetabular component beyond the confines of the native acetabulum
reveal.clinical_significance: Suggests malposition of the acetabular component
reveal.decision_impact: Should prompt consideration of revision surgery

requestables.name: Consultation on 9 August 2010
requestables.description: Details of the consultation with Mr Cope

reveal.result_summary: Mr Cope did not record or investigate the claimant's groin pain
reveal.clinical_significance: Missed opportunity to address post-operative complications
reveal.decision_impact: Failure to investigate could delay appropriate management

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Patient presents with severe pain post-surgery

timeline_phases.name: Follow-up Consultation
timeline_phases.description: Consultation with Mr Cope regarding post-operative pain

decision_points.clinical_context: During the left total hip replacement surgery
decision_points.prompt: How should the acetabular component be positioned?

options.description: Ensure acetabular component is not prominent and does not interfere with the iliopsoas tendon
options.clinical_reasoning: Avoids known complication of iliopsoas tendonitis

options.description: Position acetabular component without checking for prominence

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not ensure the acetabular component was not prominent
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant was not trained to check for prominence

expected_action_court.description: Acetabular component should have been positioned to avoid prominence
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established this as standard practice

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Acetabular component should have been checked for prominence

decision_points.prompt: What should be done when a patient reports post-operative groin pain?

options.description: Investigate the cause of the groin pain with imaging and examination
options.clinical_reasoning: Necessary to identify and address potential complications

options.description: Do not record or investigate the groin pain

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant did not record or investigate the groin pain
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant did not recall the complaint

expected_action_court.description: Groin pain should have been investigated
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Standard practice to investigate post-operative complications

explanation.what_should_have_happened: Groin pain should have been recorded and investigated

patient_outcome.description: Patient underwent revision surgery but symptoms did not fully resolve

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to ensure proper placement of acetabular component
Failure to investigate post-operative groin pain

malpractice_determination.point_of_failure: Failure to ensure proper placement of acetabular component and investigate post-operative pain
malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Proper placement and investigation could have prevented ongoing symptoms

tests_performed.result: Difference in version in the right and left hips

treatments.outcome: Symptoms improved but did not resolve

allegations.allegation: Failure to ensure proper placement of acetabular component
Failure to investigate post-operative groin pain
